name: North Sanpete
description_short: Federal credit union in Fairview, UT with 3 branches serving 351 members. Offers checking, savings, ATM/debit cards and loan products since 1959.
description_long: North Sanpete Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Fairview, Utah, that has operated for 67 years since its founding in 1959. With a membership type designated as Educational and a charter number of 13224, it serves a defined membership base in the Sanpete County region. The credit union maintains 3 branch locations and operates under federal oversight, with its most recent audit completed on June 1, 2025. The institution offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and various loan products. Based on their financial statements, the credit union provides unsecured loans and other loan products to members, with total loans of $781,656 as of their last reporting period. Members have access to traditional deposit accounts through regular shares and other deposit products, with total shares and deposits of $947,325. As a small, community-focused federal credit union, North Sanpete distinguishes itself through local presence and membership-based governance. The organization is led by CEO Jessica Clare and Chairperson Sean Rawlinson. With total assets of $1,229,115, this is a small institution focused on serving its defined membership rather than the general public. Prospective members should note that this credit union's small size means limited resources and service options compared to larger financial institutions. The institution reported net income of $3,021 in their most recent period, indicating tight operational margins. As a membership-based credit union with Educational membership designation, access may be restricted to eligible individuals rather than being open to the general public.
pros: Federal credit union with NCUSIF deposit insurance protection for member accounts
Local community focus with 67-year operating history in Fairview, Utah area since 1959
Member-owned structure provides democratic governance and profit-sharing potential through dividends
Offers ATM/debit card access and electronic banking services for member convenience
Small, personalized service model with direct access to management (CEO Jessica Clare, Chairperson Sean Rawlinson)
Provides both secured and unsecured loan products for member financing needs
cons: Very small institution ($1.23M in assets) with limited service capacity and product range compared to larger credit unions
Membership may be restricted based on Educational designation criteria—not open to general public
Only 3 branch locations limits physical access for members outside Fairview area
Tight operational margins (only $3,021 net income) may limit ability to offer competitive rates or invest in technology
Limited investment products—zero holdings in investments as of last report suggests no investment advisory services
best_for: Members of educational organizations or institutions eligible for membership at this specific credit union
Sanpete County residents seeking local, community-based banking with personalized service
Individuals prioritizing member-owned cooperative banking over corporate banking structures
diagnosis: North Sanpete Federal Credit Union is best suited for eligible members within the educational membership category seeking basic banking services and community-focused lending in Utah's Sanpete County. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted by their Educational membership designation, and as a very small institution with $1.23M in assets, they offer limited services and product options compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
